text:
Once upon a time in a small fishing village nestled along the coast, there lived a wise old crab named Old Willoughby. Every morning he would glide gracefully through the calm waters, gathering driftwood and seaweed for the community market.

On a particularly sunny day, while selling the day's catch, Old Willoughby met a young bird named Lily. She was full of questions about the sea's secrets and felt a strange connection with Old Willoughby.

They spent countless days chatting, teaching each other about life on shore and the vast world beneath the waves. One day, Old Willoughby shared a legend about a magical pearl tucked away somewhere deep within the coral gardens. The pearl was said to grant the finder the ability to communicate with all sea creatures.

Eager to try her newfound power, Lily embarked on a quest. With Old Willoughby as her guide, she searched for days under moonlight. They encountered playful dolphins, wise sea turtles, and gentle whale songs.
